question: A team adds the Camunda Spring Boot Starter and declares `@JobWorker` methods whose parameters they want bound to process variables _by name_, without specifying annotation values on each one. Per the getting-started page, what build setting must be enabled to make this work?

options.text: Enable the Java compiler flag `-parameters` (e.g. via `maven-compiler-plugin` `compilerArgs`).
Add `@EnableConfigurationProperties` to the Spring Boot application class.
Build with JDK 17 — modern JDKs retain parameter names automatically with no extra flag.

a.text: The getting-started page lists this as Step 2: enable the Java compiler `-parameters` flag so parameter names are retained at runtime and can be matched to process variables without explicit annotation values.

b.text: `@EnableConfigurationProperties` exposes `@ConfigurationProperties` beans; it has nothing to do with retaining method parameter names in bytecode.

c.text: There is no such configuration property — name binding depends on bytecode-level parameter names, which the compiler emits only when `-parameters` is passed.

d.text: The JDK version is unrelated; even on JDK 17+ the compiler strips method parameter names from class files unless `-parameters` is passed to `javac`.

explanation: By default the Java compiler does not emit method parameter names into the class file. The Spring Boot Starter needs those names to bind `@JobWorker` parameters to process variables without explicit annotation values, so the docs instruct teams to enable the `-parameters` compiler flag via `maven-compiler-plugin`, Gradle's `tasks.withType(JavaCompile)`, or the IntelliJ Java Compiler settings.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - Get started
